---
title: "Aesthetic Shiny Apps"
author: "Ray Hunter"
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%B %d, %Y')`"
format:
  revealjs:
    embed-resources: true
    # footer: "Ray Hunter, 2025"
    slide-number: true
    theme: 
      - styles.scss     
---     


<!-- What we will learn -->

[What we will cover]{.s-title} 

- Overview of web design (HTML, CSS, and Javascript)
- Packages to spice up your Shiny app 
- Integrating custom HTML + CSS

![](images/shiny.svg.png){.absolute bottom="3%" right="90%" width="15%"}
![](images/bslib.png){.absolute bottom="3%" right="65%" width="15%"}
![](images/htmltools.png){.absolute bottom="3%" right="40%" width="15%"}
![](images/thematic.png){.absolute bottom="3%" right="15%" width="15%"}

---

<div class="slide-header" style="display:flex;  gap:10px;">
  <img src="images\shiny.svg.png" alt="Shiny Logo" style="height:2.5em; width:auto; margin-top: 0rem;"/>
  <p class="s-title">Shiny can feel boring</p> 
</div>


![](images/shiny_app/shinybasic.png){.round}

---

But before we can make Shiny look nice, we need to understand the basics of web design...


---

<!-- Web Design  -->

:::: {.layout-ncol=3}

::: {.column .small width="33%"}

[HTML {{< iconify logos:html-5 size=2xl >}}]{.column-header}

- *HyperText Markup Language*
- The skeleton of the web
- Blueprints for structuring content

:::

::: {.column .small width="33%" }

[CSS {{< iconify logos:css-3 size=2xl >}} ]{.column-header}

- *Cascading Style Sheets*
- The style of the web (colors, fonts, etc.)
- Helps us make things look nice and pretty

:::

::: {.column .small width="33%" }

[JS <img src="images/js_logo.png" style="width: 42%; height: auto; display: inline-block; vertical-align: bottom; margin-left: -.9em; margin-top: -0.3em;" />]{.column-header style="vertical-align: bottom;"}

- *JavasScript*
- The interactivity of the web
- Makes things move and respond to user actions

:::

::::

![](images/webdesign.png){.round .absolute bottom="1%" right="-15%" width="25%"}

---

[HTML and CSS Basics]{.s-title} 


:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="25%"}

[HTML {{< iconify logos:html-5 size=2xl >}}]{.column-header}

- `<div>`
- `<title>`
- `<p>` 
- `<h1>`
- `<img>`

:::
::: {.column width="25%"}

[CSS {{< iconify logos:css-3 size=2xl >}} ]{.column-header}

- background
- padding
- border-radius
- font-size

:::
::::

![](images/htmltags.jpg){.round .absolute top=150 right=-100 width=50%}

---

[Basic box]{.s-title} 

```{html, eval=FALSE, echo=TRUE}
<div style="background: red; padding: 16px; border-radius: 
      12px; display: inline-block;">
<div style="background: blue; padding: 6px 10px; 
      border-radius: 8px;">
<p style="font-size: 20px; font-style: italic; 
      text-decoration: underline; background: green;">HTML and CSS</p>
</div>
</div>
```

<br>

<div style="background: red; padding: 16px; border-radius: 12px; display: inline-block;">
<div style="background: blue; padding: 6px 10px; border-radius: 8px;">
<p style="font-size: 20px; font-style: italic; text-decoration: underline; background: green;">HTML and CSS</p>
</div>
</div>

---

[Sablefish]{.s-title} 

```{html, eval=FALSE, echo=TRUE}
<div style="align-items:center; 
  border: 4px solid red; border-radius:12px; 
  padding:1rem; min-height:220px; width:30%; 
  margin:0.5rem auto 0; position:relative;">
<img src="images/sablefish.jpg" alt="Sablefish" 
    style="max-width:100%; height:auto; display:block;" />
    Sablefish
</div>

```


<div style="align-items:center; 
  border: 4px solid red; border-radius:12px; padding:1rem; 
  min-height:220px; width:30%; margin:0.5rem auto 0; position:relative;">
<img src="images/sablefish.jpg" alt="Sablefish" style="max-width:100%; height:auto; display:block;" />
    Sablefish
</div>

----


[Fortunately we don't need to do this *all* by hand]{.center} 

![](images/shiny.svg.png){style="width: 15%; height: auto; display: block; margin: 0 auto;"}


::::{.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
  - Shiny uses wrappers around `HTML`, `CSS`, and `JS` 
  - Uses **`bootstrap 3`** for styling (CSS framework with pre-designed components/styles)
:::
::::


![](images/shiny_app/shinybasic.png){.round .absolute top=275 right=-200 width="700" height="auto"}

---

But Shiny's default styling can feel a bit dated...

<br>

[How can we *avoid* writing excessive HTML and CSS?]{.s-title}

![](images/htmltags.jpg){.round fig-align="center" width=80%}

---

[`{bslib}` provides an easy solution]{.center} 

![](images/bslib.png){style="width: 20%; height: auto; display: block; margin: 0 auto;"}

- Founded on modern `bootstrap 5`
- 20+ pre-built themes (e.g. `flatly`, `minty`, `darkly`)
- Easily customize colors, fonts, and other design elements 
- Consolidates design process

---

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

![](images\shiny.svg.png){.column-header style="width: 25%; height: auto; display: block; margin: 0 auto;"}

- bootstrap 3 
- limited styling options
- css required for more customization



:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](images/bslib.png){.column-header style="width: 25%; height: auto; display: block; margin: 0 auto;"}

- bootstrap 5
- high flexibility
- `bs_theme()` for pre-built or custom themes
- uses card based layout 

:::
:::: 

---

[*Same structure, new functions*]{.center} 

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

![](images\shiny.svg.png){.column-header style="width: 25%; height: auto; display: block; margin: 0 auto;"}

- `fluidPage()` 
- `navbarPage()` 
- `tabsetPanel()`



:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](images/bslib.png){.column-header style="width: 25%; height: auto; display: block; margin: 0 auto;"}

- `page_fluid()`
- `page_navbar()`
- `page_sidebar()`
- more
    - `card()`, `value_box()`, `alert()`

:::
::::


---

![](images/shiny_app/shinybasic.png){.round fig-align="center" width=120% height="auto"}

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="25%"}

![](images/shiny.svg.png){fig-align="center" width=40%}

:::
::: {.column width="75%"}

`navbarPage()`, `tabPanel()`, `sidebarLayout()`

:::
::::

----

![](images/shiny_app/shinybasic_bslib.png){.round fig-align="center" width=140%}

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="25%"}

![](images/bslib.png){fig-align="center" width=40%}

:::
::: {.column width="75%"}

`page_navbar()`, `nav_panel()`, `layout_sidebar()`

:::
::::

---

[Looks *better*, but still a bit bland]{.center} 

[Let's try using one of the pre-built themes, `minty`]{.center} 

<br>

```{r}
#| code-line-numbers: "4-7"
#| eval: false
#| echo: true

ui <- page_navbar(
  title = "Palmer Penguins Explorer",

  # adding custom bslib theme
  theme = bslib::bs_theme(
    bootswatch = "minty" # adding a pre-built theme
  )
  # ... Other UI code...
)
```

---

[We are getting there!]{.center} 

![](images/shiny_app/minty.png){.round fig-align="center" width=100%}

---

[Lets add some custom theme on top of `minty`]{.center} 

<br>

```{r}
#| code-line-numbers: "4-14"
#| eval: false
#| echo: true

ui <- page_navbar(
  title = "Palmer Penguins Explorer",

  # adding custom bslib theme
  theme = bslib::bs_theme(
    bootswatch = "minty", # start with a pre-built theme
    fg = "#293f2fff", # change foreground color
    bg = "#e5f2fcff", # change background color
    "navbar-bg" = "#021d31ff", # navbar background
    primary = "#2f99a7ff", # accents
    base_font = font_google("Roboto"), # fonts
    heading_font = font_google("Pacifico") # fonts
  )
  # ... Other UI code...
)
```

---

[Looking better]{.center} 

![](images/shiny_app/minty_custom.png){.round fig-align="center" width=100%}

But the `ggplot` doesnt follow the theme...

---


<div class="slide-header" style="display:flex;  gap:10px;">
  <img src="images\thematic.png" alt="Thematic Logo" style="height:3.5em; width:auto; margin-top: 0rem;"/>
  <p class="s-title">dynamic ggplot theme</p> 
</div>

<br> 

```{r}
#| code-line-numbers: "3"
#| eval: false
#| echo: true

server <- function(input, output) {
  # ... Server code ...
  thematic::thematic_shiny(font = "auto") # themes ggplot to bslib
}
```



---

<div class="slide-header" style="display:flex;  gap:10px;">
  <img src="images\thematic.png" alt="Thematic Logo" style="height:3.5em; width:auto; margin-top: 0rem;"/>
  <p class="s-title">dynamic ggplot theme</p> 
</div>

![](images/shiny_app/minty_thematic.png){.round fig-align="center" width=100%}

---

[Icons and Value Boxes]{.topic}

---

[`{bsicons}` ]{.s-title} 

[https://icons.getbootstrap.com/](https://icons.getbootstrap.com/){.center}

![](images/bsicons.png){.round fig-align="center" width=100%}

---

```{r}
#| eval: false
#| echo: true
value_box(
  value = nrow(penguins),
  title = "Total Penguins",
  showcase = bsicons::bs_icon("hash"),
  theme = "primary"
)
```


![](images/shiny_app/minty_vb.png){.round fig-align="center" width=100%}

---

[`{shinyWidgets}` ]{.s-title} 

`shinyWidgets::shinyWidgetsGallery()` for full gallery

![](images/shinywidgets.png){.round fig-align="center" width=100%}

---

[HTML and CSS]{.title}

---




![](images/htmltools.png){fig-align="center" width="20%"} 



:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}

Create custom HTML elements and CSS styles directly in R Shiny with `{htmltools}`

:::

::: {.column width="40%"}

- `tags$p()`
- `tags$div()`
- `tags$img()`
- `tags$style()`

:::
::::

---

```{r}
#| eval: false
#| echo: true

htmltools::tags$img(
  src = "sablefish.jpg", # adding sablefish image to sidebar
  width = "100%",
  style = "margin-top: 1rem; border-radius: 6px;" #CSS
)
```


![](images/shiny_app/minty_sable.png){.round fig-align="center" width=100%}

---

```{r}
#| eval: false
#| echo: true

htmltools::tags$div(
  "Image: Sablefish",
  style = "font-size: 0.9rem; color: #555; text-align: center; margin-top: 0.1rem;"
)
```


![](images/shiny_app/minty_sable_text.png){.round fig-align="center" width=100%}


---

[CSS]{.title} 

---

[Heirarchy of CSS]{.s-title}


1. Inline (Highest priority)

```{r, eval=FALSE, echo=TRUE}
htmltools::tags$img(
  src = "sablefish.jpg",
  style = "margin-top: 1rem; border-radius: 6px;" #CSS
)
```

2. Internal (medium priority)

```{r, eval=FALSE, echo=TRUE}
tags$head(tags$style()) # calling CSS rules directly in the app
```


3. `External CSS (Lowest priority, but best practice)`

```{r, eval=FALSE, echo=TRUE}
tags$head(tags$link()) # calling an external CSS file out of app
```



---

## Linking external CSS file

```{r}
#| code-line-numbers: "7-12"
#| eval: false
#| echo: true
ui <- page_navbar(
  title = "Palmer Penguins Explorer",
  theme = bs_theme(
    #... Theme code...
  ),

  # Linking extrnal CSS file
  tags$head(tags$link(
    rel = "stylesheet",
    type = "text/css", # type of file
    href = "styles.css" # file name
  )),
  # ...Other UI code...
)

```

---

![](images/img_css.png){.round fig-align="center" width=100%}

---

![](images/shiny_app/inspector.png){.round fig-align="center" width=100%}

---

Targeting the **class** of an element

```{css, eval=FALSE, echo=TRUE}
.control-label { /* changing label styles */
    font-weight: bold !important;
    font-size: 1.1rem !important;
    color: #021d31ff !important;
    text-decoration: underline !important;
}

.card-header { /* changing card header styles */
    background-color: teal !important;
    color: white !important;
    font-style: italic !important;
}

.bslib-sidebar-layout>.main { /* changing main panel background */
    background-color: #021d31ff !important;
}
```

---

![](images/shiny_app/bslib_css.png){.round fig-align="center" width=100%}

---

---

## Common Selector Types

1. **Element Selector**: targets all elements of a specific type (e.g., `p`, `h1`, `div`).
2. **Class Selector**: targets elements with a specific class attribute (e.g., `.my-class`).
3. **ID Selector**: targets a single element with a specific id attribute (e.g., `#my-id`).
4. **Attribute Selector**: targets element attribute (e.g., `input[type="text"]`).
5. **Pseudo-class Selector**: targets element state (e.g., `a:hover` for links when hovered).

---
